An eccentric [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] alive nineteen [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], '''Humat''' maintained a [[Collector/Legends|collection]] of unusual, mostly sinister [[Dark side artifact/Legends|artifacts]], including the talisman known as the [[soul stealer]]. Humat stored his collection in the booby-trapped catacomb known as the [[Cell of Imu]], which was situated on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], under the [[Skyscraper/Legends|skyscrapers]] of the [[Petrax Historic Quarter|Petrax Quarter]].

==Biography==
[[File:Coruscant_view_EII_2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Humat's collection of artifacts came to rest under the skyscrapers of Coruscant.]]
Humat was an eccentric member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] who lived more than nineteen thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="Coruscant" /> during the period of [[Time/Legends|time]] known as the [[Great Manifest Period/Legends|Great Manifest Period]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the beginning and end of the [[Great Manifest Period/Legends|Great Manifest Period]] in [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000 BBY]] and [[17,000 BBY]], respectively.</ref> Humat was known for [[Collector/Legends|collecting]] unusual arcane [[Dark side artifact/Legends|artifacts]], of which many were meant for evil applications. The centerpiece of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi's]] collection was the [[soul stealer]], a [[Milk/Legends|milky]]-[[Color/Legends|white]] talisman that attempted to possess any nearby individuals and compel them to try to [[Death/Legends|kill]] anyone they saw.<ref name="Coruscant" />

Humat stored his collection on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in the catacomb known as the [[Cell of Imu]]. To protect his artifacts from [[Thief/Legends|thieves]], Humat installed dozens of deadly traps in the chamber, such as trapdoors, tripwires, and ceiling spikes, as well as dried corpses of animals that were animated through [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Magic/Legends|magic]] and made to attack any intruders. Over the subsequent [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]], the Cell of Imu became buried under the rising [[Skyscraper/Legends|skyscrapers]] of the [[Petrax Historic Quarter|Petrax Quarter]]. At some point, however, Humat's legendary chamber was uncovered when a [[Construction droid/Legends|construction droid]] razed one of the tall buildings, allowing [[Archaeologist/Legends|archaeologists]] to access the Cell of Imu and the artifacts contained within.<ref name="Coruscant" />

==Behind the scenes==
Humat was mentioned in the [[2003]] sourcebook ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'', which was published by [[Wizards of the Coast]] for use with the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Coruscant" />
